title: Cleome raphanoides
taxonomy: Cleome raphanoides is classified in the kingdom Plantae, in the family Cleomaceae. It belongs to the order Brassicales and the superorder Rosanae. Within the family Cleomaceae, it is placed in the tribe Cleomeae. The species is further categorized into the genus Cleome, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as bee plants or spider flowers. Cleome raphanoides is a monotypic genus, meaning it is the only species within its genus.
uses: Cleome raphanoides is known for its medicinal properties in traditional medicine. It has been used to treat various health issues, including fever and digestive problems. In some cultures, it is used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and skin conditions. The plant's roots and leaves are often harvested for their medicinal value. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Cleome raphanoides in treating these conditions.

side_effects: Cleome raphanoides is known to cause skin and eye irritation upon contact, and it may also lead to allergic reactions in some individuals. intro: Cleome raphanoides, also known as bee nut or bee plant, is a flowering plant in the Cleomaceae family, native to the Americas. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Cleome raphanoides include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cleome raphanoides include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat colds, fever, and skin conditions.
